get.description:
Redirect the browser to GitHub's authorization endpoint.

Generates a fresh ``state`` token (CSRF) and PKCE pair, stores them in
the server-side session, then issues a 302 redirect.

Handle the GitHub OAuth callback.

Validates ``state``, exchanges the authorization code for an access token,
fetches the user identity, encrypts the token, and stores it in
``oauth_connections``.
Return ``{connected, user_identity}`` for the GitHub OAuth connection.

delete.description:
Revoke the GitHub OAuth token and remove it from the database.

Calls the GitHub token revocation API (best-effort) before deleting the
local ``oauth_connections`` row.

get.description:
Redirect the browser to Atlassian's authorization endpoint.

Uses Atlassian's OAuth 2.0 3LO (Three-Legged OAuth) flow with PKCE.
``offline_access`` scope is requested to obtain a refresh token.

Handle the Jira OAuth callback.

Validates ``state``, exchanges the authorization code for access + refresh
tokens, fetches the ``cloud_id``, encrypts the tokens, and stores them in
``oauth_connections``.
Return ``{connected, user_identity, cloud_id}`` for the Jira OAuth connection.

delete.description:
Revoke the Jira OAuth token and remove it from the database.

Calls the Atlassian token revocation API (best-effort) before deleting the
local ``oauth_connections`` row.

get.summary: Combined OAuth status for all providers
get.description:
Return the OAuth connection status for all providers.

Response shape::

    {
        "github": {"connected": bool, "user_identity": str | null},
        "jira":   {"connected": bool, "user_identity": str | null, "cloud_id": str | null},
        "provider_mode": "vendor" | "customer"
    }

get.description:
Get all available repositories from GitHub tokens.
Returns deduplicated repos based on full_name, showing all repos
that any token has access to.

When an OAuth connection is active and no repos are cached yet, performs
an on-demand discovery against the GitHub API before returning results.

Get all available Jira boards/projects from Jira credentials.
Returns deduplicated boards based on project_key, showing all boards
that any credential has access to.

When an OAuth connection is active and no projects are cached yet, performs
an on-demand discovery against the Atlassian Cloud API before returning results.

get.description: Returns the current license status, as determined by the background task.

post.description:
Trigger developer metrics computation.

Behavior:
- If `github` is provided (a `GithubRepoKey`), compute PR metrics for that repo.
  Optional `author` filters results to that author.
- If `jira` is provided (a `JiraProjectKey`), compute ticket metrics for that project.
  Optional `assignee` filters results to that assignee.
- If neither `github` nor `jira` provided, compute metrics for all repos/projects.

Trigger QA metrics computation.

Behavior:
- If `jira` is provided (a `JiraProjectKey`), compute QA metrics for that project.
  Optional `creator` filters results to that creator.
- If `jira` is not provided, compute QA metrics for all projects found in `jira_issues`.

Run HR import (optional) and reconciliation (optional).
- Upload either CSV files (people_file and roles_file) OR pass hr_endpoint URL returning HR JSON.
- min_conf: minimum confidence (0..1) to persist mapping.
- dry_run: if true, do not write DB, return candidate matches instead.

get.description:
Ingest HR data (optional) and reconcile external identities.

Summary:
- Ingest: fetches CSV (or stream) data as configured, maps source headers to canonical
  columns, transforms rows and optionally persists people and roles to the local DB.
- Reconcile: scans stored analytics tables for external ids (GitHub/Jira),
  scores candidates against people records, and optionally upserts identity links
  when confidence >= min_conf.

Parameters:
- body (dict): JSON body containing optional sections "people" and "roles" (each either
  an ingestion config or a lightweight mapping description). Body keys can override
  query flags.
- import_hr (query): enable ingestion step (default True). Overridden by body["import_hr"].
- reconcile (query): enable reconciliation step (default True). Overridden by body["reconcile"].
- min_conf (query): confidence threshold to persist identity links (default 0.7).
  Overridden by body["min_conf"].
- dry_run (query): if True, do not write to DB; return counts only. Overridden by body["dry_run"].

Returns (JSON):
- imported_people, imported_roles: counts persisted (or transformed counts if dry_run).
- scanned: dict with counts scanned per system {"github": n, "jira": m}.
- persisted: number of identity links written (zero if dry_run).
- candidates_count, candidates_sample: reconciliation candidates (sample limited).
- dry_run, min_confidence, note: operation metadata.

Behavior/Notes:
- Sections without required canonical headers or that fail mapping/transform are skipped.
- Writes occur only when import_hr/reconcile are enabled and dry_run is False.
- Function is resilient: individual failures are logged/ignored and processing continues.

get.summary: List managers with id, full name, and role
get.description:
Returns a list of unique managers (id, full_name, role).

- No filters: returns all functional and reporting managers.
- user given: returns managers associated with that resolved user.
- role given: returns only managers of that role column.
- Both given: applies both filters.

get.summary: Get Manager Review Form By Key

get.description:
Fetch review forms by filter parameters.

At least one of ``ui_key``, ``user_id``, or ``scope_id`` should be
supplied to avoid returning every row in the table.

When ``is_draft=true``, only draft records (``is_draft=1``) are returned,
and any record that has a later submitted version (``is_draft=0``) for the
same (ui_key, user_id, scope_id) combination is excluded.

When ``is_draft=false``, only submitted records (``is_draft=0``) are
returned, and any record that has a later draft version (``is_draft=1``)
for the same combination is excluded.

When ``is_draft`` is omitted, all records are returned.

Compute a final weighted score for a person by combining AI evaluation metrics
and the individual's saved review (ratings). Returns breakdown and contributing rows.

post.description: Submit (finalize) a manager review for a user.
Save a manager review as a draft.

get.description:
Fetch manager reviews by filter parameters.

At least one of ``user_id``, ``manager_id``, or ``ui_key`` should be
supplied to avoid returning every row in the table.

When ``is_draft=true``, only draft records (``is_draft=1``) are returned,
and any record that has a later submitted version (``is_draft=0``) for the
same (user_id, manager_id, ui_key, scope_id) combination is excluded.

When ``is_draft=false``, only submitted records (``is_draft=0``) are
returned, and any record that has a later draft version (``is_draft=1``)
for the same combination is excluded.

When ``is_draft`` is omitted, all records are returned (existing behaviour).

Retrieve saved manager review drafts for a given ``ui_key``.

Returns the most recently updated draft if found, or an empty list when none
exist.  Optionally filter by ``user_id``, ``manager_id``, and ``scope_id``.
